CVE-2026-34743: Buffer overflow in lzma_index_append()
XZ Utils 5.6.0 (2024-02-24) and 5.6.1 (2024-03-09) release tarballs contain a backdoor that was inserted by a malicious co-maintainer.
It was discovered by Andres Freund and made public on 2024-03-29.
A patch to fix it was made public on 2022-04-07.
The releases have been signed with Lasse Collin’s OpenPGP key.
git clone https://github.com/tukaani-project/xz
The GNU Autotools-based build is old, feature complete, and the most tested.
CMake-based build became feature complete in June 2024.
For GNU Autotools, it is recommended to use the latest versions.
From the version 5.5.2beta onwards, the core components of XZ Utils are under the BSD Zero Clause License (0BSD).
Some parts of XZ Utils (for example, scripts from GNU gzip and some build system files) are under different free software licenses such as GNU LGPLv2.1, GNU GPLv2, or GNU GPLv3.
If a security issue has been discovered, please report it privately via email (encrypted if possible) or use the Report a vulnerability button in the security section on GitHub."},{"url":"https://optimizedbyotto.com/post/xz-backdoor-debian-git-detection/","title":"Could the XZ backdoor have been detected with better Git and Debian packaging practices?","content":"The following command will download all Debian source packages of xz-utils from Debian release 5.2.4-1 onwards:
gpg: key 38EE757D69184620: public key "Lasse Collin <lasse.collin@tukaani.org>" imported
$ sha256sum xz-utils_5.8.1.orig.tar.xz /tmp/xz-5.8.1.tar.xz
`diffoscope --html-dir xz-utils-5.6.4_vs_5.8.0 xz-utils_5.6.4.orig.tar.xz xz-utils_5.8.0.orig.tar.xz
* a39f3428 Correct the nocheck build profile
| * a522a226 Bump version and soname for 5.8.1
| * 48440e24 Tests: Add a fuzzing target for the multithreaded .xz decoder
| * d5a2ffe4 liblzma: mt dec: Don't free the input buffer too early (CVE-2025-31115)
| * 831b55b9 liblzma: mt dec: Fix a comment
| * c8e0a489 DOS: Update Makefile to fix the build
| * 307c02ed sysdefs.h: Avoid <stdalign.h> even with C11 compilers
| * 4f52e738 Translations: Partially fix overtranslation in Serbian man pages
An online example repository with only a few missing uploads added using`gbp import-dsc` can be viewed at salsa.debian.org/otto/xz-utils-2025-09-29/-/network/debian%2Funstable"},{"url":"https://www.heise.de/en/news/XZ-Utils-5-8-3-Security-update-with-unclear-risk-11244670.html","title":"XZ Utils 5.8.3: Security update with unclear risk | heise online","content":"The security vulnerability CVE-2026-34743 affects XZ Utils from version 5.0.0 onwards.
The developers are not releasing new versions of the 5.2.x, 5.4.x, or 5.6.x development branches, but have incorporated the bug fixes into the xz Git repository.
Two years ago, the compression library xz was the cause of a near-disaster."},{"url":"https://codeberg.org/tukaani/xz/releases","title":"Releases - tukaani/xz","content":"Dec 17, 2025 — XZ Utils 5.8.2 Stable Larhzu released this 5 months ago. Fix the build on ARM64 on glibc versions older than 2.24 (2016). This avoids a ..."}]}

The package should be updated to follow the last version of Debian Policy (Standards-Version 4.7.4 instead of 4.7.3). * [2026-04-06]  Accepted xz-utils 5.8.3-1 (source) into unstable  (Otto Kekäläinen). * [2025-12-26]  Accepted xz-utils 5.8.2-1 (source) into unstable  (Sebastian Andrzej Siewior). * [2025-09-04]  Accepted xz-utils 5.8.1-1.1 (source) into unstable  (Matthias Klose). * [2025-04-05]  Accepted xz-utils 5.4.1-1 (source) into proposed-updates  (Debian FTP Masters) (signed by: Sebastian Andrzej Siewior). * [2025-04-05]  Accepted xz-utils 5.4.1-1 (source) into stable-security  (Debian FTP Masters) (signed by: Sebastian Andrzej Siewior). * [2025-03-26]  Accepted xz-utils 5.8.0-1 (source) into experimental  (Sebastian Andrzej Siewior).","score":0.6929958,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://github.com/tukaani-project/xz/releases","title":"Releases · tukaani-project/xz","content":"XZ Utils 5.8.3 (stable)  Latest. **IMPORTANT:** This includes a fix for CVE-2026-34743 which affects all XZ Utils versions since 5.0.0. No new 5.2.x, 5.4.x, or 5.6.x releases will be made, but the fix is in the v5.2, v5.4, and v5.6 branches in the xz Git repository. 5.8.3 (2026-03-31) * liblzma: - Fix a buffer overflow in lzma_index_append(): If lzma_index_decoder() was used to decode an Index that contained no Records, the resulting lzma_index was left in a state where where a subsequent lzma_index_append() would allocate too little memory, and a buffer overflow would occur. In the few applications that do use these functions, the combination of function calls required to trigger this bug are unlikely to exist, because there typically is no reason to append Records to a decoded lzma_index. - Fix the build on Windows ARM64EC. * xz: - Fix invalid memory access in --files and --files0. It was already enabled in the CMake-based build. * xz-5.8.3-windows.7z. * xz-5.8.3-windows.7z.sig. * xz-5.8.3.tar.xz.","score":0.6304352,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://sourceforge.net/projects/lzmautils/files/xz-5.8.2.tar.gz/download","title":"Download xz-5.8.2.tar.gz (XZ Utils)","content":"Download XZ Utils xz-5.8.2.tar.gz. Does this version (5.6.1) still contain the detected vulnerability? Please note that the latest available version of xz-utils is version 5.8.3. > hsE4mrnvvv1v9RSc wrote: Tue Jun 02, 2026 8:17 am I just noticed that the version of xz-utils installed with Linux Mint 22.3 is 5.6.1. As Ubuntu, from which Linux Mint inherits xz-utils, backport security fixes to older package versions, you may have to check whether the security vulnerability is listed as fixed in the changelog. Based on this Ubuntu Security article, your xz-utils package 5.6.1 should be safe, i.e. patched: USN-7414-1: XZ Utils vulnerability, also: Ubuntu on CVE-2025-31115. xz-utils (5.6.1+really5.4.5-1ubuntu0.3) noble-security; urgency=medium. the worker thread in src/liblzma/common/stream_decoder_mt.c. xz-utils (5.6.1+really5.4.5-1build0.1) noble-proposed; urgency=medium. xz-utils (5.6.1+really5.4.5-1) unstable; urgency=critical.","score":0.4368388,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://tukaani.org/xz","title":"XZ Utils","content":"# XZ Utils. XZ Utils are a complete C99 implementation of the .xz file format. XZ Utils were originally written for POSIX systems but have been ported to a few non-POSIX systems as well. The core of the XZ Utils compression code is based on LZMA SDK but it has been modified significantly to be suitable for XZ Utils. * **liblzma** is a compression library with an API similar to that of zlib. * **xz** is a command line tool with syntax similar to that of gzip. In XZ Utils 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0, the multithreaded .xz decoder in liblzma has a bug where invalid input can at least result in a crash. When built for native Windows (mingw-w64 or MSVC), the command line tools from XZ Utils 5.6.2 and older have a command line argument injection vulnerability. See the XZ Utils backdoor page for more information and also the XZ Utils review notes. XZ Utils 5.8.3 fix a minor security issue that affects all stable XZ Utils releases.","score":0.41321722,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.44,"request_id":"39584c09-d73c-4c71-93bc-388955d8c0f6"}
